channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris and Trump hold competing rallies in Michigan
item.description: While the two candidates have been crisscrossing the swing states for weeks, this is the first time they are literally crossing paths, with each of them holding events in the suburbs north of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea, Asma Khalid, and Eric McDaniel, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin US Senate candidates trade accusations of lying during testy debate
item.description: Democratic U.S. Sen. Tammy Baldwin and Republican challenger Eric Hovde have repeatedly accused each other of lying during an often testy and confrontational debate.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris are campaigning for votes in pivotal Michigan
item.description: With the Nov. 5 election fast approaching, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump are setting their sights on the key battleground state of Michigan on Friday. The vice president began her day in Grand Rapids before holding events in Lansing and Oakland County, northwest of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Big bets on Trump don’t mean the market is rigged: Betting analyst
item.description: Michael Schwimer says a flood of bets for Trump can’t rig the markets any more than a big bet on the World Cup will affect how the game is played.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Facing a budget deficit challenge, Johnson aims to safeguard youth and workforce
item.description: Enticing grocery store chains to open in underserved communities is another priority for the Johnson administration.
item.dc:creator: Julian Crews, WGN 9

item.title: Exclusive: Chauncey Parker talks new role as NYC deputy mayor for public safety
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Chauncey Parker to be New York City’s new deputy mayor for public safety. Parker takes over the position that was held by Philip Banks, who resigned the week prior.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: A former Park Service ranger says U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Home stretch: Harris, Trump hold dueling events, rallies in crucial battleground state
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Trump held dueling campaign events in battleground Michigan with less than three weeks to go until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's gone silent on Ukraine support, ranking member of Armed Services Committee warns
item.description: Roger Wicker, the head of the Senate’s armed forces committee, urged President Biden to aggressively accelerate aid to Ukraine in his final weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump earned money from government sources through Washington hotel, Democrats find
item.description: Over 11 months while former President Trump was in office, his Washington, D.C., hotel took in some $300,000 from sources inside and outside of government, including tens of thousands in payments from the U.S. Secret Service.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: President Biden renews push for ceasefire talks
item.description: President Joe Biden, as he left Germany, says the U.S. will restart ceasefire and hostage release negotiations, after the Israeli military killed Hamas' leader, Yahya Sinwar.
item.dc:creator: Basil John, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What's a 'Jezebel spirit'? Some Christians use the term to paint Kamala Harris with a demonic brush
item.description: Christian nationalist leaders are telling followers that Vice President Kamala Harris is under the influence of a “Jezebel spirit,” using a term with deeply racist and misogynistic roots that is setting off alarm bells for religious and political scholars.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI-generated images have become a new form of propaganda this election season
item.description: This election cycle, AI-generated images have proliferated on social media platforms after politically charged news events. They often spread partisan narratives rather than facts.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: Harris and Trump seek Arab American votes in Michigan in effort to shore up battleground states
item.description: Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are both seeking support from Arab American voters as they campaign in Michigan, trying to lock down support in a battleground state that could decide the presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Kamala Harris said Thursday that Israel's killing of Hamas' top leader offers "an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza," talking plainly about next steps even as the pro-Palestinian demonstrators who gathered outside her campaign event underscored the complicated politics at p
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, COLLEEN LONG and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: SBA administrator visits Tampa Bay as disaster loan program runs dry
item.description: While visiting businesses in Tampa Bay, which included both of those businesses, SBA Administrator Isabel Casillas Guzman said the next move is up to members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about absentee, mail-in, and early voting
item.description: Various forms of casting election ballots have changed since the COVID-19 pandemic affected in-person turnout and voting
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump visits barbershop in the Bronx
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) – Former President Donald Trump visited a barbershop in the Bronx on Thursday. Trump stopped by Javiel's Barbershop on Castle Hill Avenue.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways from the last full fundraising quarter before Election Day
item.description: Federal candidates have submitted their reports for what was the final full fundraising quarter before Election Day, offering a snapshot of their financial status with just three weeks to go.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: How to watch the Nevada US Senate Debate
item.description: The only debate between Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown will occur on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other eligible workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance says 'no,' he does not think Trump lost in 2020
item.description: Vance has faced repeated questions on the campaign trail about whether Trump lost in 2020. On Wednesday, he gave his most direct answer to date, answering "no" in response to a reporter's question.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Harris interview on Fox gets testy — but also gives her a do-over
item.description: The interview was the vice president's first formal sit-down with Fox. She faced questions on immigration, and was given a chance to answer again what she would do differently from President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Florida elections officials seek flexibility on voting rules after hurricanes
item.description: Florida's recent hurricanes could make it difficult for voters in affected areas to cast their ballots in the upcoming election unless Gov. Ron DeSantis waives and modifies some rules, county election supervisors said in a letter to state officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lorena Biasotti, CCSD school board candidate, discusses priorities ahead of election
item.description: Lorena Biasotti, a mother of four and candidate for Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, spoke about her platform on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ag leaders voice concerns over negative impacts of tariffs proposed in presidential election
item.description: Midwestern agricultural leaders say they are concerned new tariffs being suggested by former President Donald Trump as part of his reelection bid could result in a damaging trade war. They say it could cost the corn and soybeans billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Trump spends big on anti-trans ads for 2024 election
item.description: Trump spends more on ads about transgender women and girls in sports and gender transitions than on Bidenomics, fracking and taxes.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: The 3rd Precinct keeps showing up in the GOP playbook. Minneapolis wants to turn the page
item.description: Speaking outside the former 3rd Precinct in Minneapolis this week, Republican vice presidential candidate JD Vance criticized Gov. Tim Walz for his response to civil unrest in 2020, painting a picture of a burned-down city with rampant crime.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Mormon voters set to play critical role in swing states
item.description: Mormon voters are increasingly seen as a crucial swing vote in the presidential election, with both Vice President Harris and former President Trump stepping up their outreach to the demographic in multiple battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada political parties seek to sway ‘Gen Z’ amid inflation and recovery from an on-campus mass shooting
item.description: Young Democrats and College Republicans at UNLV are campaigning for their respective candidates in the 2024 election, citing issues such as inflation, gun violence, and reproductive freedoms, while the Trump campaign is targeting students through social media and face-to-face meetings.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In close Minn. House races, personal problems old and new can be vexing for candidates
item.description: With the Minnesota House majority hinging on a small number of seats, flaws in candidate pasts are resurfacing as parties and groups jockey for an edge that can decide key races.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump addresses Economic Club of Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO — Former President Donald Trump is back in Chicago Tuesday. Trump addressed the Economic Club of Chicago at Fairmont Chicago, located at 200 North Columbus Drive, ahead of the Nov. 5 election.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WGN 9
